Masar Boulevard · 3.65 km

A shaded, climate-managed urban spine connecting Al Haramain Rail to Al Haram.

Ultra-Premium Destination Retail · 318,000 sqm

A luxury retail environment comparable to leading GCC retail districts — luxury maisons, premium outlets and the largest mall in Makkah.

الحَـرَم · The Haram

Where worship now meets daily life

For over 1,400 years of Islamic history, visitors came to Al Haram solely to perform the rites of worship.

For the first time, Al Haram is also a place to live, work, dine and shop — including from a rooftop restaurant with a direct view of the Kaaba.

Masar Makkah operationalises this transformation through a regulated, master-planned, Royal Decree founded development — turning faith-driven demand into a long-duration, hard-asset investment thesis.

🕌

Pray

550 m to Masjid Al-Haram on foot.

🍽️

Dine

Rooftop dining with direct Kaaba sightlines.

🛍️

Shop

Premium retail anchored by global luxury brands.

🏠

Own

Generational ownership inside Al Haram.

Investment Thesis

Structurally irreplaceable — by design, not by narrative

Sovereign backing, captive demand and an irreplaceable location combine into a once-in-a-generation, institutional-grade real estate thesis.

i.

Captive, Inelastic Demand

Approximately 30 million pilgrims annually — government-managed and constitutionally protected. Demand is structurally decoupled from global economic cycles.

ii.

2 Billion Muslim Buyer Pool

Following recent regulatory reform, non-Saudi Muslims may now own real estate in Makkah for the first time — unlocking the largest faith-anchored buyer pool ever made addressable to a single asset class.

iii.

Trophy Asset Inside Al Haram

Not adjacent to Al Haram — inside it. Properties of this nature are typically held across generations, supporting a structural floor on intrinsic value.

iv.

Sovereign A/Stable Backing

Royal Decree foundation, embedded in Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030. The Kingdom is rated A/Stable (S&P).

v.

Asymmetric Forward Upside

Current phase: asset monetisation. Future phase: 40,000+ keys at stabilised occupancy is expected to transform the platform into a recurring-yield generator.

vi.

Defensible Demand to Yield

Al Haram draws pilgrim flows largely independent of macro conditions — supporting a yield profile that few global real estate platforms can credibly underwrite.

Masar Makkah is structurally irreplaceable — the convergence of sovereign backing, non-cyclical demand, an irreplaceable location and hard-asset collateral.
Masar Towers · A New Skyline

Fifty-eight hospitality towers and eighty-two residential towers — a new skyline for the Holy Capital.

Project Brief

A 4.8 km linear urban development

Redefining pilgrimage, hospitality and urban life in Makkah — PIF-backed, founded by Royal Decree, certified LEED Gold for Communities.

Strategic Location

Heart of Makkah with direct access to Al Haram — among the most coveted addresses in the global Muslim real estate market.

🏗️

Institutional Developer

PIF-backed Umm Al-Qura, listed on Saudi Exchange, founded 2012 by Royal Decree. Sole owner and operator of all 205 master-planned plots.

🏙️

Mixed-Use Programme

The most transformative urban development in Makkah — hospitality, commercial, residential, healthcare and premium retail.

🚌

Connectivity

100 m to Al Haramain High-Speed Rail, integrated metro and BRT, 40,000 underground parking spaces and a 3.65 km shaded boulevard.

🗺️

Three-Zone Masterplan

Al Haram, Central and Gate Zones — each with distinct programmatic offerings. LEED Gold certified for Communities.

🔑

Diversified Product Mix

~24K hotel keys, ~13K residential units, 59 service-apartment towers, a 200-bed hospital and 318K sqm of retail.

🏨 Hospitality

The Developer

Umm Al-Qura — founded by Royal Decree

Publicly listed on the Saudi Exchange. Sole owner, developer and operator of all 205 plots within the Masar master plan.

SAR 14.4 Bn
Paid-In Capital
SAR 2.9 Bn
FY2025 Revenue (+59% YoY)
SAR 983 Mn
FY2025 Net Profit (+97% YoY)
74 / 205
Plots Contracted
SAR 2.15 Bn
Operating Cash Flow (5×)
2012

Founded by Royal Decree

Closed JV in Makkah established with initial capital of SAR 916 million and a mandate to develop the western corridor of the Holy City.

2012 — 2017

Phase 1 — Land Clearance

Over 3,600 properties cleared, with more than SAR 10 billion in compensation paid. Foundation for an institutional-grade master plan.

2017 — 2023

Phase 2 — SAR 22 Bn Infrastructure

Roads, tunnels and full utilities delivered. All 205 plots fully serviced. SAR 22 billion of an SAR 24 billion programme completed.

2022 +

Phase 3 — Sales & Vertical Development

74 plots contracted (36% of inventory). 22 transactions in 2025. FY2025 revenue of SAR 2.9 billion (+59% year-on-year).

2025

Capital Base Raised to SAR 14.4 Bn

Net profit SAR 983 million (+97% YoY). Operating cash flow SAR 2.15 billion — a 5× expansion. Platform now in full commercial operation.

State-of-the-Art Infrastructure

SAR 22 Billion already deployed

Over twelve years of execution: all 205 plots serviced, roads delivered, tunnels excavated, utilities connected. The capital-intensive groundwork is complete.

SAR 22 Bn+ Infrastructure

All 205 plots are serviced and ready for vertical development. Masar's smart-city infrastructure is engineered to a 100-year operational horizon, materially de-risking subsequent phases of construction.

Opportunity Structure

A joint venture between HAP & NAEEM

An institutional partnership combining best-in-class development execution with regional capital structuring and investor access.

Hassan Allam Properties
Lead Developer

Institutional development execution, deep delivery track record, master-planned community expertise.

×
NAEEM Holding Group
Co-Developer · Sponsor · Capital Partner

Real estate arm acting as co-developer, sponsor, capital structuring partner and investment platform partner.

The opportunity is contemplated to be developed through a joint venture between Hassan Allam Properties (HAP) and the real estate arm of NAEEM Holding Group. HAP is expected to act as lead developer, bringing institutional development execution capabilities, while NAEEM's real estate arm is expected to act as co-developer, sponsor, capital structuring partner and investment platform partner, leveraging NAEEM Holding Group's regional investor access and capital markets expertise.

The Opportunity

Branded residence JV via fund structure

A 4,948 sqm plot in Zone 2 — five minutes to the Mall, five minutes to Al Haramain Station, seven minutes to King Abdullah Mosque.

Project Cost Build-Up

Land (4,948 sqm)39%SAR 414 M
Construction Costs48%SAR 510 M
Soft Costs13%SAR 143 M
Total Project Cost100%SAR 1.066 Bn

Sources of Funding

In-Kind Equity18%SAR 189 M
Cash Equity23%SAR 240 M
Off-Plan Sales23%SAR 243 M
Senior Debt37%SAR 395 M
Total Sources100%SAR 1.066 Bn
📐

BUA Breakdown

Total built-up area of 86,000 sqm — of which 56,000 sqm is dedicated to residential product.

⏳

Project Duration

Three-year construction and sales programme designed to minimise holding cost and equity drag.

🏗️

Development Model

Balanced stack of equity, senior debt and off-plan sales — meaningfully reducing execution and capital risk.

🔒

WAFI-Linked Collections

Buyer collections tied to construction completion milestones — protecting investor capital and end-buyers.

💹

Fund Structure

Returns realised at fund termination. No interim dividends — value compounds to a single liquidity event.

🏛️

Regulated Vehicle

Investment contemplated through a regulated fund structure with institutional governance and oversight.

Feasibility & Indicative Returns

Indicative ~40% IRR · 2.7× cash on cash

Indicative figures only. Subject to financial model validation, due diligence, structuring, market conditions and final financing terms.

~40%
Indicative Internal Rate of Return

Underwritten at SAR 40,600 / sqm average selling price — sensitivity analysis indicates ~42% IRR at SAR 42,000 / sqm.

Avg Selling Price / sqm
SAR 7.2K

SAR 1.95 Bn
Indicative Project Sales
SAR 650 Mn
Total Development Costs
2.7×
Cash on Cash Return
Optimised capital stack — equity, senior debt and off-plan sales combined to enhance capital efficiency through measured leverage and pre-sales de-risking.
Termination return profile — no interim distributions during fund life; value compounds to exit through a single, structured liquidity event.
WAFI compliance — collections tied to construction milestones, providing regulatory and counterparty protection for investor capital.
Upside scenario — at SAR 42,000 / sqm, indicative returns rise to ~42% IRR — illustrative, not a representation of expected outcomes.
Hard-asset floor — land represents 39% of total cost (SAR 414 M) — providing a tangible value anchor regardless of execution pace.
Institutional governance — regulated fund vehicle with independent reporting, audit, and investor protection frameworks.
IMPORTANT — INDICATIVE FIGURES ONLY. All returns, IRR ranges and cash-on-cash multiples shown above are indicative and presented for discussion purposes only. They are subject to validation of the underlying financial model, completion of full investor and project due diligence, final transaction structuring and capital stack composition, prevailing market and regulatory conditions, and the terms of senior debt and off-plan sales financing. None of the figures constitute a forecast, projection, representation, warranty or guarantee of future performance. Actual returns may differ materially. This material does not constitute an offer, solicitation or recommendation to invest.
Risk Factors & Mitigation

A responsible investment thesis

A balanced view of identified risk factors and the structural mitigants embedded in the Masar platform.

Risk FactorLevelMitigant
Execution & ConstructionMediumRisk distributed across 200+ plots and independent developers. Master-plan infrastructure already complete — the most capital-intensive phase has been delivered.
Delivery Timeline SlippageMediumDelays in individual towers do not impair platform-level value. Land retains intrinsic worth independent of build pace.
Revenue ConcentrationMediumCurrent revenue base weighted to land sales. As hospitality assets come online (2026+), recurring income is expected to diversify the revenue mix materially.
Pilgrimage Quota RiskLowVision 2030 targets 30 million pilgrims by 2030. Quotas have expanded consistently over time.
Geopolitical / SovereignLowSaudi Arabia rated A/Stable (S&P). Asset located within Islam's holiest city — disruption risk is among the lowest globally for this asset class.
Financing & Rate RiskLowUmm Al-Qura reduced reliance on external financing in 2025, supported by SAR 2.15 Bn of operating cash flow.
Overall risk profile: Low. The combination of sovereign backing, non-cyclical demand, an irreplaceable location and hard-asset collateral represents one of the more defensive risk-return frameworks available in the global real estate market today.
Strategic Distribution Partner

Coldwell Banker — Global Sales & Marketing Engine

A distribution engine — not a broker. Mandated to convert global Muslim wealth into qualified, governed investor flow through digital acquisition, an activated 40+ country broker network, curated private events and a fully tracked sales funnel.

Global Distribution Network
40+ Countries · 100K+ Professionals
Strategic Distribution Engine

How Sales Are Delivered

Coldwell Banker is mandated to convert global Muslim wealth into controlled, qualified investor flow — across digital, broker and private channels — under a single governance framework.

Execution combines performance marketing (search, social, programmatic), an activated 40+ country broker network, curated private investor events, an institutional data room and a fully tracked lead-to-conversion funnel — protected by phased releases and price-floor discipline.

40+
Countries
100K+
Professionals
100 Yrs
Heritage

The Distribution Engine

01

Digital Acquisition

Performance marketing across search, social and programmatic — calibrated to faith-aligned, high-net-worth audiences in tier-one Muslim wealth markets.

02

Broker Network Activation

Mobilisation of Coldwell Banker's 40+ country broker network with controlled allocations, sales kit deployment and incentive alignment.

03

Investor Funnel

Lead capture → KYC and pre-qualification → suitability review → reservation → conversion. CRM-tracked, governed and reportable end-to-end.

04

Roadshows & Private Events

Curated investor roadshows and private salons across Riyadh, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Doha, Cairo, London and Singapore.

05

Data Room & Sales Process

Institutional-grade virtual data room, structured documentation flow, follow-up cadence and conversion tracking — fully governed.

06

Pricing & Phased Release

Phased inventory releases, allocation discipline and price-floor protection to preserve trophy positioning and absorption velocity.

Target Buyer Segmentation

Six addressable buyer pools

A structured segmentation of the qualified buyer and investor universe most likely to absorb premium product at Masar Makkah.

i.

Non-Saudi Muslim Buyers

Primary segment unlocked by recent regulatory reform — first-time access to Makkah real estate ownership for non-Saudi Muslims globally.

ii.

GCC HNWIs & Family Offices

High-net-worth individuals and family offices across the Gulf seeking trophy assets aligned with faith-anchored generational wealth strategies.

iii.

Egyptian & Regional Investors

Investors seeking SAR-denominated exposure as a hard-currency hedge with regional cultural and religious affinity.

iv.

Muslim Diaspora

Diaspora buyers across Europe, Asia and North America seeking a permanent connection to Makkah and a tangible faith-linked asset.

v.

Religious Tourism Investors

Investors targeting the structural growth of the Hajj and Umrah hospitality economy and its long-duration income profile.

vi.

Legacy & Inheritance Buyers

Multi-generational buyers acquiring Makkah property as a legacy asset — held across generations rather than traded.

Marketing & Investor Activation

A disciplined go-to-market framework

An institutional sales and marketing plan engineered to support absorption, pricing discipline and investor quality.

  • 01
    Investor Pre-QualificationKYC, suitability and accreditation review at first point of engagement.
  • 02
    GCC & Regional RoadshowsTargeted institutional and HNWI roadshows across Riyadh, Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Doha and Cairo.

    Digital Investor CampaignsProgrammatic and content-led campaigns calibrated to qualified, faith-aligned audiences.

    Broker Network ActivationMobilisation of Coldwell Banker's network across 49 countries.

    Premium StorytellingNarrative anchored on scarcity, proximity to Al Haram, and trophy-grade product positioning.

    Off-Plan Sales FunnelStructured launch phasing tied to construction milestones and WAFI compliance.

    Investor Data RoomInstitutional-grade virtual data room with structured documentation and follow-up.
  • 08

Investor FAQ

Frequently asked investor questions

A reference framework for institutional and qualified-retail diligence conversations.

A branded residence development on a 4,948 sqm plot in Zone 2 of Masar Makkah. Total project cost indicatively SAR 1.066 Bn, three-year horizon, structured through a regulated fund vehicle.

Qualified institutional and sophisticated investors meeting jurisdictional accreditation: sovereign wealth, family offices, HNWIs / UHNWIs, asset managers and faith-aligned institutional capital.

Four characteristics rarely found together: sovereign Royal Decree backing, non-cyclical pilgrim demand, irreplaceable location inside Al Haram, and hard-asset land collateral. Recent reform now permits non-Saudi Muslim ownership — materially expanding the buyer base.

Indicative IRR ~40% with 2.7× cash-on-cash, underwritten at SAR 40,600/sqm. Sensitivity at SAR 42,000/sqm: ~42% IRR.

Indicative only — subject to model validation, due diligence, structuring, market conditions and financing terms. Not guaranteed.

Regulated fund vehicle. Capital stack: cash equity, in-kind equity, senior debt and off-plan sales. Returns realised at fund termination — no interim dividends; value compounds to a single liquidity event.

NAEEM's real estate arm acts as co-developer, sponsor, capital structuring partner and investment platform partner — leveraging regional investor access and capital markets expertise.

Hassan Allam Properties (HAP) acts as lead developer — institutional execution, delivery track record, master-planned community expertise.

Strategic global sales and marketing engine — not a broker. Mandated to deliver controlled absorption, pricing discipline and qualified investor flow across 40+ countries.

Identified: execution, timeline slippage, revenue concentration, pilgrimage quotas, geopolitical / sovereign, financing. Mitigants: completed infrastructure, sovereign backing, hard-asset land floor, diversified developer base, conservative leverage. Full risk pack in the data room.

Primary exit through off-plan and post-completion unit sales over the three-year horizon, with residual liquidity at fund termination. Secondary placements and platform-level events may be considered subject to market conditions.
